channel.title: Nature
channel.description: Nature is the foremost international weekly scientific journal in the world and is the flagship journal for Nature Portfolio. It publishes the finest peer-reviewed research in all fields of science and technology on the basis of its originality, importance, interdisciplinary interest, timeliness, accessibility, elegance and surprising conclusions. Nature publishes landmark papers, award winning news, leading comment and expert opinion on important, topical scientific news and events that enable readers to share the latest discoveries in science and evolve the discussion amongst the global scientific community.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Structural modifications in strain-engineered bilayer nickelate thin films
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 01 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10446-2

Structural modifications in strain-engineered bilayer nickelate thin films
item.dc:title: Structural modifications in strain-engineered bilayer nickelate thin films
item.dc:creator: Lopa Bhatt
Edgar Abarca Morales
Abigail Y. Jiang
Eun Kyo Ko
Yi-Feng Zhao
Noah Schnitzer

Dan Ferenc Segedin
Yidi Liu
Yijun Yu
Charles M. Brooks
Antia S. Botana
Harold Y. Hwang
Julia A. Mundy
David A. Muller
Berit H. Goodge

item.title: These advanced solar cells have an antique source: old bullets
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 01 April 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01001-0

Lead recycled from centuries-old ammunition provides raw materials for perovskite solar cells.
item.dc:title: These advanced solar cells have an antique source: old bullets

item.title: Moiré engineering of Cooper-pair density modulation states
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 01 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10325-w

Researchers created a moiré superlattice in Sb2Te3/FeTe bilayers, producing spatially modulated superconducting gaps directly imaged with Josephson scanning tunnelling microscopy and spectroscopy, tunable by replacing Sb2Te3 with Bi2Te3.
item.dc:title: Moiré engineering of Cooper-pair density modulation states
item.dc:creator: Zihao Wang
Bing Xia
Stephen Paolini
Zi-Jie Yan
Pu Xiao
Jiatao Song
Veer Gowda
Hongtao Rong
Di Xiao
Xiaodong Xu
Weida Wu
Ziqiang Wang
Cui-Zu Chang

item.title: Stoichiometric FeTe is a superconductor
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 01 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10321-0

Analysis of FeTe films grown using molecular-beam epitaxy and annealed under a Te flux post-growth shows that stoichiometric FeTe is inherently a superconductor, contradicting the long-held view that it is an antiferromagnetic metal.
item.dc:title: Stoichiometric FeTe is a superconductor
item.dc:creator: Zi-Jie Yan
Zihao Wang
Bing Xia
Stephen Paolini
Ying-Ting Chan
Nikalabh Dihingia
Hongtao Rong
Pu Xiao
Kalana D. Halanayake
Jiatao Song
Veer Gowda
Danielle Reifsnyder Hickey
Weida Wu
Jiabin Yu
Peter J. Hirschfeld
Cui-Zu Chang

item.title: Nanoscale transfer-printed full-colour ultrahigh-resolution quantum dot LEDs
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 01 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10333-w

A dual-action force dynamics strategy using a hard silicon template as a nanoimprinting stamp combined with inverted transfer printing is described for the manufacture of high-performance full-colour ultrahigh-resolution quantum dot light-emitting diodes (LEDs) for active-matrix displays, while revealing electric-field reconstruction in nanoscale arrays and introducing dielectric matching to mitigate field concentration and performance degradation.
item.dc:title: Nanoscale transfer-printed full-colour ultrahigh-resolution quantum dot LEDs
item.dc:creator: Lihua Lin
Jie Wang
Hailong Hu
Haolin Luo
Yanbin Liu
Xingjie Yang
Jingnan Su
De’er Li
Zhongwei Xu
Chengyu Luo
Yongshen Yu
Tailiang Guo
Fushan Li

item.title: Angle evolution of the superconducting phase diagram in twisted bilayer WSe<sub>2</sub>
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 01 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10357-2

Superconductivity in twisted bilayer WSe2 evolves smoothly with twist angle and emerges near Fermi surface reconstruction, linking previously distinct phase diagrams and clarifying its correlated origin.
item.dc:title: Angle evolution of the superconducting phase diagram in twisted bilayer WSe<sub>2</sub>
item.dc:creator: Yinjie Guo
John Cenker
Ammon Fischer
Daniel Muñoz-Segovia
Jordan Pack
Luke Holtzman
Lennart Klebl
Kenji Watanabe
Takashi Taniguchi
Katayun Barmak
James Hone
Angel Rubio
Dante M. Kennes
Andrew J. Millis
Abhay Pasupathy
Cory R. Dean

item.title: Dual-symmetry-guided assembly of complex lattices
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 01 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10364-3

A dual-symmetry-guided strategy is used to assemble a broad class of complex Archimedean lattices and two-dimensional quasicrystalline structures, providing a general and experimentally accessible route to complex-symmetry materials.
item.dc:title: Dual-symmetry-guided assembly of complex lattices
item.dc:creator: Huang Fang
Xiaotian Li
Wensi Sun
Chengxin Wang
Nuo Chen
Yining Gan
Jiping Huang
Yuqiang Ma
Hajime Tanaka
Peng Tan

item.title: Author Correction: Signatures of ambient pressure superconductivity in thin film La<sub>3</sub>Ni<sub>2</sub>O<sub>7</sub>
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 31 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10335-8

Author Correction: Signatures of ambient pressure superconductivity in thin film La3Ni2O7
item.dc:title: Author Correction: Signatures of ambient pressure superconductivity in thin film La<sub>3</sub>Ni<sub>2</sub>O<sub>7</sub>

item.title: Static electricity is a mystery but invisible carbon may be key
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 30 March 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01012-x

A thin layer of carbon on the surface of oxide grains changes how they exchange static charge.
item.dc:title: Static electricity is a mystery but invisible carbon may be key
item.dc:creator: Shamini Bundell
